title: Preparing grape-juice agar plates for Drosophila embryo collections
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"6rain","text":"This protocol details the preparation of grape-juice agar plates, yielding 80 to 100 plates in 35 mm petri dishes for the collection of Drosophila embryos. These plates are intended to be affixed to plastic Drosophila bottles (6 oz and 8 oz). For optimal results, we recommend using 8 oz bottles, as their firmer plastic ensures a more secure plate fit. This method is suitable for collecting embryos intended for downstream processing or to count embryos laid from a group of flies. If measuring embryo-lay rates from individual pairs, we recommend starting with this protocol: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":136,"length":10},{"style":"italic","offset":207,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7hre8","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"bkb4g","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"protocols","mutability":"IMMUTABLE","data":{"id":218148,"title":"Preparing fly-food spoons for Drosophila embryo collection","title_html":"\u003cp\u003ePreparing fly-food spoons for \u003cem\u003eDrosophila\u003c/em\u003e embryo collection\u003c/p\u003e","load_collapsed":true,"uri":"preparing-fly-food-spoons-for-drosophila-embryo-co-gzacbx2ax","type_id":1,"image":{"source":"https://files.protocols.io/external/tqa5b38u7-F2D06FA7687511F08F960A58A9FEAC02.jpg","webp_source":null,"placeholder":"https://files.protocols.io/external/tqa5b38u7-F2D06FA7687511F08F960A58A9FEAC02.jpg","webp_placeholder":null},"guid":"4C54C9CDA54140D5A34053DF4A1EAC25","creator":{"name":"J. steps: Measure the following ingredients using a graduated cylinder and precision balance, and combine in a 1 L media bottle: 350 mL diH 2 O 12.5 g Drosophila Agar, Gelidium, 100 Mesh Genesee Scientific Catalog #66-104

While waiting for the autoclave: measure the following ingredients in order using a graduated cylinder and precision balance, and combine in a 250 mL Erlenmeyer flask: 0.25 g Tegosept, Fly Food Preservative Genesee Scientific Catalog #20-259 10 mL Ethanol, 200 Proof KOPTEC Catalog #DSP-MD-43 150 mL Welch's 100% Concord Grape Juice Swirl the flask to combine. It is expected that the Tegosept will only partially dissolve.
While waiting for the autoclave: align clean 35 mm petri dishes on a clean work surface.

Add the contents of the 250 mL flask containing grape juice to 1 L flask. Mix well by swirling until the color of the grape juice is evenly distributed. When pouring, allow the solution from the 250 mL flask to flow slowly along the inner wall of the 1 L flask. This technique helps prevent bubble formation.
Loosely cap the media bottle containing the agar/water solution. Affix a piece of autoclave tape to the top of the bottle.

Place the flask in an autoclave bin filled with tap water. Autoclave the media bottle containing the agar/water solution at 121 °C for 00:30:00 . Placing the media bottle in an autoclave bin with a few inches of tap water helps maintain the agar solution's warmth after autoclaving. This extended heat retention provides additional working time for pouring the plates (as described below).

Using a serological pipette with 50 mL tip, distribute the agar-grape-juice solution into 35 mm petri dishes. Fill each plate completely and avoid introducing bubbles to the plates. Plates containing bubbles are usable; however, flies commonly lay their embryos within the resulting depressions. This can complicate subsequent embryo collection and accurate counts.
Allow the plates to cool at Room temperature .
Once cool, plates are ready to use. To store plates for later, collect into stacks of 25, wrap in cling film, and keep at 4 °C until use. Plates can be stored for up to 2 weeks.
creator: J. Dylan Shropshire
